articles.title: As five big U.S. banks report earnings on the same day, Citigroup is the one to watch
articles.description: Among the largest U.S. banks, Citigroup is expected to show the greatest improvement by one important measure. But it still has a long way to go to reach its own performance target.

articles.title: Earnings estimates have been following an unusual pattern this time around
articles.description: Analysts generally lower their estimates in the months before earnings. But thanks to the energy and tech sectors, expectations have actually climbed heading into second-quarter results.

articles.title: The No. 1 decision for aging retirees: Stay at home or move into a senior community?
articles.description: Why staying in your longtime home requires more planning than you think.

articles.title: The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
articles.description: As earnings season kicks off, AI investment remains front and center.

articles.title: My insurance company said my roof lost a few tiles. Loss adjusters found $10,000 in storm damage. How could this happen?
articles.description: “My house shook violently from the wind.”

articles.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
articles.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”

articles.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
articles.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

articles.title: Is it better to spend my savings now so I can delay taking Social Security? How do I choose?
articles.description: “By claiming earlier, I could preserve more of my portfolio and allow those assets to continue compounding.”

articles.title: Your data built the AI boom — but Big Tech is pocketing 100% of the equity
articles.description: Your share of the AI wealth is a right — not a handout. Here is how we claw back our money.

articles.title: Only 5% of U.S. adults can ace this 8-question financial-literacy test. Can you?
articles.description: Financial literacy just hit a 10-year low — and it is devastating bank accounts.

articles.title: 'Funflation' hits home: Why staying in isn't the cost-saver it used to be
articles.description: Following a wave of price hikes, at-home pastimes like streaming movies or playing video games are pinching pocketbooks.

articles.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
articles.description: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

articles.title: China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high
articles.description: Investors increasingly view China's two-speed growth with robust export and tepid domestic demand as a defining long-term feature of the Chinese economy.

articles.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
articles.description: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics.

articles.title: U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%
articles.description: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

articles.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
articles.description: A historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

articles.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
articles.description: Nonfarm payrolls are projected to post a gain of 115,000, according to the Dow Jones consensus.

articles.title: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June, less than expected, ADP reports
articles.description: Companies added slightly fewer workers than forecast, with hiring targeted heavily toward healthcare-related sectors.

articles.title: North Sea oil and renewables: The UK’s next PM faces a defining energy policy decision

articles.title: Do you really, really love your job? Then you're not alone, according to surprising results from this survey

articles.title: China widens Japan export curbs, targeting drone makers, nuclear firms and defense institutes
articles.description: China blacklisted four of Japan's government defense research institutes and placed dozens more Japanese firms under tightened export restrictions Monday.

articles.title: Shipping rebounds in Strait of Hormuz one week after U.S.-Iran deal – but fragile confidence threatens recovery

articles.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation plan after first vessel attack under peace deal

articles.title: Trump pledges rapid U.S. response for Venezuela after historic earthquakes kill hundreds

articles.title: Iran warns ships it's 'unacceptable and dangerous' to transit the Strait of Hormuz without their approval
articles.description: The stern warning underscores Tehran's resolve to retain control over the Strait of Hormuz and to resist transits that bypass its authorization.

articles.title: Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows

articles.title: Factory job cuts in June neared financial crisis and Covid levels, S&P says
